creator: asomers@FreeBSD.org
text: I'm using TrueOS beta.  fish 2.2.0 worked fine.  But after upgrading to fish 2.3.1, the backspace key is ignored and the arrow keys enter "[D", "[A", "[C", and "[B".  Delete, Home, End and Page Up/Down also enter similar garbage.  Oddly, these keys work fine virtual terminal.  Also, bash works fine with QTerminal 0.6.  So the problem seems to be an interaction between QTerminal and Fish 2.3.1.  I've verified that rebuilding Fish 2.2.0 from ports fixes the problem.  Next, I'll try bisecting the upstream source to find the cause of the bug.
